## [00:00 - 03:02]

Now that we've brought together our campaign building by understanding our Strategy our avatar building our funnels building our audiences and our creatives the next part is now Starting your testing. So this section is all about how I build my testing how I structure my testing ready for me to analyze my ads and audiences to find the winners and Then how I take those winners and graduate them Graduation testing is something I'll be covering off which is my way of running and testing to maximize Performance. So let's just take a step back and look at this graphic here, which I'm sure you're familiar with right now This is how the option works. So the advertiser value the customer the consumer the user experience and therefore the value That you are scored at in the auction now One of the challenges when you're running testing is if you have a stable Campaign, let's say for example, you're spending whatever it's $500 5,000 50,000 dollars a day and you've got that across one or many campaigns And if the performance is going well and you go to introduce a new creative You want to know that that creative is going to perform because the worst thing you can do and it is something I did many many years ago is you take a Campaign that's performing well and you drop an ad in there that has not been tested So you don't know whether this ad is any good and bear in mind an ad is only as good as It appears for the audience. So if you have let's say Five different ad sets and you have some ad sets targeting some interests some ad sets here Targeting some lookalikes etc. There's no guarantee that the one ad that you have is going to work across all audiences It's just the way that it works and the same happens to you if for example You draw your ad on a piece of paper and walk the street and show it to lots different people Even if they're in your target market, even if they're looking to buy your product They might see your ad and say yeah, it doesn't really appeal to me That's kind of how it works on Facebook as well so what I want to do with my testing is to try and overcome the guesswork and Focus on creating ads and audiences through my test process that are going to give me better performance when I go into Prospecting and scaling and the way I do that is to focus on ads that Give me Insight that they're going to work for this particular audience and therefore the confidence that when I go and use that ad for my Existing campaigns that I have a higher chance of it working and really the goal of the testing is that I Can win the best auctions now Let me just define what I mean by the best auctions the best auctions are the cheapest and They're the highest converting and they're the highest value for me

## [03:02 - 06:01]

So if you're running e-com for example, you want low-cost traffic You want high conversion rates on your site and you want a high average order value Obviously down the line you want good lifetime value as well, but just talking about the Facebook auction Facebook know which type of people are higher high like have a high likelihood of Purchasing for example, so if you create a website conversion objective and you optimize your ad set for purchase That's you telling Facebook that no matter what audience I put into this ad set I want the people that are more likely to commit the purchase event Doesn't always mean buy like one of the things about this is Facebook can only work on the data you're sending it So if you're saying I want people that buy is based on people hitting that purchase event on many other websites as well So let's say you have an audience and for the sake of argument It's 50 million people and you say to Facebook right from that 50 million people in my ad set I want to target everyone that's a purchaser. So you set that to website conversion. You set the event to purchase What Facebook does is it looks at those 50 million people and says how many of these people are likely to buy? It could be It could be half a million It could just be 500,000 people at Facebook is identified now The problem is you look at the audience and you say there are 50 million people that can potentially sell to Facebook says well, there's only 500,000 that we have identified that are in market and are likely to buy your product You don't know this necessarily and so you might be running ads Scaling doing really well and then all of a sudden your performance stops giving you results You're looking at and thinking what couldn't be the audience because there's like 50 million people here I've only generated 10,000 sales. I've hardly tapped into the audience Maybe even you've done a hundred thousand sales, but the reality is there's only 500,000 active buyers in that market What I try to do with winning the auction is to win as many of them as possible now if you're you know going back to Looking at what goes into the auction itself If your website and your customer journey is not performing well Therefore your estimated action rates are low and if your ads and the experience of those ads is poor Then you're not going to get the premium users So when I talk about winning the best auctions if I have for example 500,000 people that Facebook have identified as being the purchasers But the quality of my ads my funnel my offers my audiences everything is poor I'm not going to get the highest score I can in the auction someone else is and therefore I'm going to be dealing with slightly lower quality audiences that might be Low have a lower intense lower

propensity to convert or may not actually have it as high average order values for example or You know there's gonna be something about that auction that because now I'm missing out on the primary graduation testing now, you know when I look at the auction itself There's a few different things that you need to understand about how the auction works and why my testing process works so well So the first thing is the best auctions identify the user intent So even if you've got five hundred thousand people in any given audience that are highly likely to purchase It doesn't mean that as soon as they see your ad they're gonna buy let's say for example We take a customer journey of Someone who wakes up in the morning checks Instagram checks Facebook then you know gets themselves ready It's goes to work Then they don't look at the phone the photo their mobile again Then maybe at lunchtime they log into Facebook again just to see what their friends are doing Maybe in the you know, they have a coffee break and they're on Instagram, etc They're consuming content and they might even see your ad but they're not buying anything They're just browsing and getting on with their day then let's say for example They they get home in the evening. They have dinner there's checking fit Facebook or Instagram during the day Then later on in the evening as they're sitting in front of the TV watching Netflix They're now browsing again, maybe on their laptop maybe on the mobile But now they they remember this ad that you showed them earlier on in the morning And the ad comes up again in the news feed and like oh, I'm gonna click it now They click the ad and within 10 minutes that bought your product now You might look at that and say My analytics shows that in the evening I get more conversions So logically I should not advertise until the evening so I can save a ton of money I can maximize my return that's been correct as I've just explained You've potentially missed hitting them earlier in the day and priming them so the best auctions

## [09:02 - 12:07]

And at Facebook know this so when they're delivering your ads and sometimes you're seeing you know This time in the morning is really poor performance. I'm gonna stop advertising and all that and stuff You're actually damaging your chance of winning the auction by over optimizing so the best auctions Facebook knows when to deliver your ad To the right user at the right time and and that's a big thing about timing So you you know when I look at how best to Optimize my spend or do bidding or lifetime budget versus daily budget That all comes down to understanding how the auction works and maximizing value one of the Cool things about using lifetime budgets where you set a budget and an end date So for example, you might set a budget of five thousand dollars and run it for 14 days That doesn't mean you're gonna spend that whole amount straight away What Facebook is trying to do is to get you the best conversions through that 14-day period because Facebook's working on That what's best for you as an overall when you use daily budgets What you're telling Facebook is I kind of don't care What my performance is as long as I spend my budget like that's the main thing you're telling Facebook is spend my budget The secondary thing is you want results whereas when you do using a lifetime budget You're saying it's actually really important to me that you get me results and then spend the budget however You see fit now if you just think about that logic it makes so much sense, right? There are pros and cons of it and we'll cover that as part of the different parts of the training But it's so important to understand this about the option then the cost, you know, it's cheaper to Reach people that don't click and buy ads then it is to reach people that do click and buy the conversion path You know, maybe someone clicks into ads at lunchtime buys in the afternoon and also the value as well So, you know the cost to I did to target someone who By is at least five or six times a month Maybe clicks Facebook ads maybe buys outside of it the value of that person to you When you're optimizing for purchases is higher because they're more likely to buy from you Therefore potentially the cost is higher. So don't don't be caught into thinking that low CPM low cost is the best thing for you Sometimes a higher CPM can also work out as well. So the final part of this Section is then really giving you the overview of how the graduation framework Actually works and it's a real kind of underlying I guess architecture or structure of how this whole program is set up So the BPM method is a methodology of bringing brand and performance marketing together Then you've got elements like 5W avatar call for which we've covered building your for funnel system creating great ads And then you get into the stage of Testing so once you've got your ads you have an idea of your funnel and the audiences You've built your ad account the testing phase is like the most central part of what I do
